questions.question: What components are needed for the TF-3000?
questions.ground_truth: The TF-3000 requires Siemens PLC, Bosch Rexroth hydraulics, custom heating elements, and a vacuum forming station.
contexts: TF-3000 BOM: Siemens S7-1500 PLC, Bosch Rexroth hydraulic unit (CYH-400), custom ceramic heating elements (x12), vacuum forming station VFS-2000
TF-3000 assembly sequence: frame welding → hydraulic install → electrical → heating elements → vacuum station → programming → trial

questions.question: Which vendor has the longest lead time and what impact does it have on production?
questions.ground_truth: Siemens has the longest lead time at 10-12 weeks for electrical panels. This is the critical path — panels must be ordered 14 weeks before assembly start to avoid production delays.
contexts: Vendor: Bosch Rexroth — hydraulic components, lead time 6 weeks
Vendor: Siemens — electrical panels and PLCs, lead time 10-12 weeks
Critical path: Siemens panels must be ordered 14 weeks before assembly start

questions.question: What is the production sequence for a PF1?
questions.ground_truth: The PF1 production sequence is: frame fabrication → hydraulic installation → electrical wiring → PLC programming → trial run → FAT → dispatch.
contexts: PF1 assembly sequence: frame fabrication (2 weeks) → hydraulic install (1 week) → electrical wiring (2 weeks) → PLC programming (1 week) → trial run (3 days) → FAT (1 day) → dispatch

questions.question: Compare the PF1 and PF2 in terms of capacity and cycle time
questions.ground_truth: The PF1 has 500T capacity with 12s cycle time, while the PF2 has 800T capacity with 15s cycle time. The PF2 offers 60% more capacity but is 25% slower per cycle.
contexts: PF1 Specifications: Capacity 500T, Working area 2000x1500mm, Cycle time 12s
PF2 Specifications: Capacity 800T, Working area 2500x2000mm, Cycle time 15s
